summary: The July 13 scout screened the current Monday arXiv listings plus the latest Friday postings still inside the effective arXiv catch-up window. The strongest heavy-ion-relevant item is a QCD small-x/high-energy-evolution calculation of running-coupling effects in anti-collinear resummation, relevant to saturation and initial-state modeling. The strongest AI/ML or computational-transfer items are a commissioned low-latency GNN calorimeter trigger at Belle II, neural quantum states for medium-mass nuclei, deep Gaussian processes for graph-structured multi-fidelity models including heavy-ion emulation, and optimal-transport resampling for pathological Monte Carlo event weights.
strongest_paper_summary: arXiv:2607.09337 is the strongest heavy-ion-relevant theory paper because it tackles running-coupling effects in resummed high-energy QCD evolution, a foundational ingredient for small-x and initial-condition modeling. arXiv:2607.09347 is the strongest practical AI/ML systems paper because it reports an operational GNN trigger module with microsecond-scale latency and bit-accurate FPGA validation.

papers.title: Running coupling effects in the anti-collinear resummation in high energy evolution
papers.authors_short: Alex Kovner, Michael Lublinsky, Maxim Nefedov, Vladimir Skokov
papers.abstract: The paper studies running-coupling effects in anti-collinear resummation of JIMWLK evolution in the linear BFKL regime, derives a resummed BFKL kernel with running-coupling effects in both the DGLAP-like resummation equation and the JIMWLK kernel, and finds a reduced Pomeron intercept in the anti-collinear regime.

papers.title: Commissioning and Low Latency Operation of the Graph Neural Network Electromagnetic Calorimeter Trigger at the Belle II Experiment
papers.authors_short: M. Neu, F. Baptist, I. Haide, Y. Unno, J. Becker, et al.
papers.abstract: Reports commissioning and operation of Belle II's GNN electromagnetic calorimeter trigger module, including integration with the first-level trigger, slow control, online monitoring, hardware-algorithm co-design, 1.053 microsecond total latency, and bit-accurate agreement between RTL simulations and the offline reference model.

papers.title: Medium-mass nuclei with neural quantum states
papers.authors_short: Bryce Fore, Jane Kim, Alessandro Lovato, Anthony Tropiano
papers.abstract: Uses variational Monte Carlo with Pfaffian-Jastrow neural quantum states to compute ground-state energies and charge radii of light- to medium-mass nuclei up to A=58, while testing interaction terms inspired by pionless EFT and analyzing computational scaling.

papers.title: Deep Gaussian Processes on Directed Acyclic Graphs
papers.authors_short: Federico L. Perlino, Oliver Hamelijnck, Adam M. Johansen, Theodoros Damoulas
papers.abstract: Develops deep Gaussian processes over directed acyclic graphs with theory for prior collapse and graph-topology effects, plus a structured variational approximation. The empirical demonstrations include a multi-fidelity heavy-ion collision emulation task where the model recovers low-fidelity contributions and simulator-hierarchy structure.

papers.title: Optimal-Transport-Based Cell Resampling for Negative and Pathological Event Weights
papers.authors_short: Regan Doherty, Lauren Hay, Rishabh Jain, Matt LeBlanc, Julia Marrinan, Camille Mauceri, Jennifer Roloff
papers.abstract: Studies optimal-transport metrics, including Energy Mover's Distance and a spectral variant, for cell resampling of negative or pathologically large Monte Carlo event weights. In NLO QCD samples, the approach reduces observed bias relative to earlier cell-resampling techniques and proposes Cross-Section Mover's Distance as a bias metric.

papers.title: Entropy-Constrained Machine Learning with Residual Data Augmentation for Modeling Chemical Kinetics
papers.abstract: Introduces a thermodynamics-constrained surrogate for turbulent reacting-flow chemistry, enforcing non-negative entropy generation and using residual-based synthetic data augmentation to improve parametric extrapolation in flame DNS.

papers.title: Neutrino-induced hyperon final-state interactions as constraints on the in-medium hyperon potential / In-medium hyperon potentials and the quarkyonic hyperon onset
papers.abstract: The linked papers propose accelerator-neutrino hyperon final-state interactions as constraints on in-medium hyperon potentials and propagate those constraints into hyperon-onset and neutron-star scenarios, including comparisons to terrestrial and heavy-ion priors.
